title: Rebellion 222 Parallels
wikitext:
[]: Rebellion/2.22 Parallels             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 11, 2014 2:06 pm Parallels seem to be all the rage lately, and I seem to have spotted one from the third PMMM movie. MAJOR PMMM SPOILERS AHOY. DO NOT READ THIS POST FURTHER IF YOU DO NOT WISH TO BE CORRUPTED. Back at the end of the series, Madoka sacrifices herself to make sure that everybody will be safe from becoming witches. In this case, Madoka is our Rei and "everybody" is our Shinji. Rei sacrifices herself so that Shinji will never have to pilot an Evangelion again. Shinji gets depressed as all hell about this, and decides he wants Rei back so badly that he oh-so-heroically gets in his Eva and saves her. Of course, in the Madoka parallel, this becomes Homura becoming the queen bitch and dragging Madoka to her, regardless of what Madoka wanted. Shinji may have saved Rei from Zeruel, but it may have not been that heroic. Both Shinji and Homura had somebody they loved, who sacrificed themselves for the other's sake. Both of them couldn't take it, and dragged them back from the path that they had chosen for themselves, no matter how selfish it was. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 11, 2014 5:49 pm Yeaaaah no, sorry but I don't see any similarities between 2.0 and Rebellion, for a lot of reasons : - In PMMM Madoka's sacrifice accomplished something great, it liberated the Magical Girls from the curse of becoming a witch(well, more like sidestepped the problem, but you see my point). While in Rebuild Rei's sacrifice accomplished jack-shit. Worse : it gave Zeruel a way to infiltrate Lilith's Chamber without activating the self-destruct mechanism, so Shinji didn't had any choice but to intervene and pilot, or Misato, Ritsuko and the Bridge Bunnies would had been reduced to piles of ashes, the world purged from the Lilin and Rei lost forever(remember that now, except maybe Gendo, Fuyu and Kaji, one knew that SEELE dispatched Mark.06 to clean behind Zeruel) - In Rebellion Homura knew perfectly well what would happen if she stole Madoka's powers, hell she even precisely rewrote the rules of the universe so it would fit perfectly for Madoka's happiness and safety from the Incubators. In Rebuild Shinji didn't had any idea what his Eva would do if he tried to save Rei, he didn't even knew that his Eva was able to trigger an Impact. - We don't know exactly the scope of Homura's change on the laws of Cycle, but she says that she only snatched a fragment of it, implying that the Law of Cycle is still working. And frankly if the second season reveal that the Wraith system and Law of Cycle still works, then I would be all for Homura. Sure what she did was callous, but it worked well for everyone. Except the Incubators, but fuck them. Now in Rebuild, even if we don't know the full extend of Third Impact nor Shinji's responsibility on it, but whatever happen fucked at least the whole Hakone region hard and surely killed a lot of people, ultimately absolutely nothing good came out of what Shinji did at the end of 2.0, since Rei wasn't even saved. If anything, instead of a parallel of 2.0, Rebellion is more of a mirror image, a bizarro land where the perpetrator's consciousness and consequences are reversed.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:30 pm Now I'm not familliar with Madoka, but there couldn't be a grosser distortion of what Shinji did in 2.0 That shit went wrong doesn't have to mean that it's some sort of karmic punishment. Sometimes things go to hell because of unforseen factors beyond your control. See also how this is adressed in Q. Instead of having someonerub in Shinji's face that he didn't care about Rei or something, (quite the opposite happens with the "paying atention to the books" thing and him being shown to have meant what he told her at the end of 2.0, which was in itself a reprise of something he was shown to notice, which is Rei's wistful, tragic wish for freedom (compare fishtank scene, it's exactly the same line. )) See also the BGM, about freedom, performed by her VA. They could have had him not noticing any difference with ReiQ and order her to read the books and act as he pleases; Things he narkedly does not. Instead, we see Shinji struggle with the same idea that good actions always lead to good consequences and vice versa.. "But I saved someone, that was supposed to be a good thing, how did things turn out so fucked up?", the gist/point being more that shit that happened happened and needs to be dealt with, regardless of how/why it happened (Kaworu: "That [you didn't know shit] may be, but the others still see it that way.") WILLE finding a convenient scapegoat is markedly portrayed as making it all worse in the end. If you look at the 2.0 CR, you'll see them refer to the scene as being about salvation and having been chosen over the alternatives because they "wanted to end the movie on a more hopeful note" And there's Tsurumaki bitching about him not getting to include more ReiIII, so they did seem to have at least considered letting her live, at least for a while. Then, of course, that earthquake hit and yeah adressin national tragedies is one way to make relevant art, and change for the sake of change has been done plenty of times in this movie series already, Rei's apparent survival itself was such a move, so I guess that like a virtual particle, it returned to nothingness as easily as it popped into existance, or maybe they were deliberately setting us up to break our hearts; We might know when the 3.0 CR comes out, or never at all. I mean, what "chosen path" would Shinji be taking away from her, anyway? From a lonely death thinking she will only ever be the means, never the end, and that freedom is impossible to obtain? What principles of hers did he exactly violate? If anything, he's trying to measure up to her. He knows he's not perfect; The point isn't that he should be. He already realized he shouldn't have left fter bardiel, and with how things have turned out, he might never forgive himself for that. Of course she, in turn, was showing her gratefulness to him by making dinner for him for once; It's called a mutually benefical relationship where two people inspire each other to grow beyond their limits. You might as well twist that logic around and say that Rei was "not giving a fuck about what Shinji would think" when she took on Zeruel for his sake or something. Because that's what Shinji was trying to do here, make her the end, not the means for once in her life. If you look at the scene itself, the dialogue is all about Rei; They don't even have Shinji say something like, "I need you" or any huge elaborate declarations of what he thinks, it's all, "Thank you for all your effors" [that he's tying to repay her/measure up to with this] and "You are most definitely NOT expendable!" Because when you look at Rei, it doesn't take that much to brighten her day a bit, she can value the little things; But at the same time, she didn't often actively seek her own happiness. When it came her way on its own, like when Gendo showed concern over her, but she never thought of seeking it out as an objective - because she "knows" what her objectives are, and it's a list dictated by Gendo. She has been living with these feelings of loneliness and of feeling trapped for a long time and never done anything about it, she simply let Shinji march out of Tokyo-3, and it's not that she didn't want him to stayshe clearly misses him right away (see when she picks uf his SDAT player while looking sad), but thinks that if he won't have to suffer anymore because of his piloting job, that's ood nough for her(see her speech during the battle) It's NOT like Rei doesn't have wishes or desires of her own; It's not like she wants to die with Zeruel; We got a few statements on what she wants, it involves being happy together with the people she cares about. She also seems to have a wistful kind of longing for freedom, hobbies, and a calling she'd probably like to pursue; and she'd die for it is necessary, but as long as angels get killed she might as well survive. She's just ruling that these are unimportant, because... we don't know if Naoko told her in this iteration, but Gendo most likely still told her she exists for his plan. What is she actually putting before her own survival in this scene? Actually, Shinji's safety. The whole exchange went like this: Ritsuko: "staph, you might get yourself killed!" Shinji: "I don't mind; I can't let any more people get hurt because I didn't fight, least of all Rei." Rei: "Don't bother getting yourself in danger for me, I'm not worth it. I can be replaced". Shinji: "You don't have to put me before yourself, I think you're important." She's saying why leaving her wouldn't change anythin, not why she would want to be left there, or why Shinji shouldn't get her out. Just listed to her gasp when she sees what he's willing to do for her... That's not someone who'se interests were just showed aside, that's not someone who's conflicted, but still obliges what their friend wants them to do - she's touched. He didn't know he would tang; He didn't know he would survive getting Rei out... or even the wounds inflicted by Zeruel earlier, which he's making all worse by moving around. Heck, if the power was off at this point, he shouldn't have been affected by the attack. (Like in the classic series) Whatever he did next, it was already sort of in progress by the moment Zeruel pierced the EVA, so even that happened to him because he wanted to save Rei. From his PoV, it should have been quite concievable that he would save Rei, bleed to death right afterwards, and not get to "enjoy" her at all. Of course you might say, "Rei might be worth something, but something at all =/= leveling the planet". Yes. But Shinji didn't intend to level the planet, nor did he know that could even happen - all Ritsuko warned him about is that he could die. Well, it's his life to throw away, and it sure speaks for the respect that Misato has (or well, used to have) for this kid when she rules him capable of conciously making such a decision. And without knowing that the EVAs can do impacts, what's the worst that could have happened? He might have ripped Zeruel to shreds an found no tace of Rei - the threat would still be neutralized. Or, consider this: Assume things had gone like in episode 20, (or 1.11 , which Shinji would have as a reference) with the EVA freakyness just stopping, with no impact, but them still being unable to retrieve Rei. Which would have served her less? Dying with Zeruel as a stragically sacrificed pawn, in confirmation of her percieved expendibleness, or after being shown proof that people value her and are willing to go through great pains for her, even if it's still futile? The latter looks like a bit of a completed character arc to me. In the end, both of them were still strategically sacrificed pawns by Gendo, and I'M not saying one girl dying happy masures up to billions dying screaming, but neither of them knew. I'm talking solely about Rei and her mindset

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:38 pm All of which I agree with, but is nothing to do with Madoka - Rebellion . But then in my view it couldn't be, because I don't see any parallel at all beyond "new episode subverts ending of previous one", which of course happens all the time in art and drama!

[]:             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Tue Feb 11, 2014 9:27 pm The only thing Rebellion maybe has anything in common when it comes to eva is EoE, but not really story wise, just how those stories were handled.
